sentences.text: Hey, Nicole, how are you doing?
Hi there.
I'm good.
I hope you are.
I'm doing well too.
I appreciate you jumping on.
Yeah.
What part of the, uh, the country are you in?
I am in North Carolina.
Oh, no, wait, what part
in, in the triad?
In Greensboro?
Very cool.
I, uh, my, my, my father and his family, they, they grew up in Durham.
Um, but I still go to the Outer Banks, uh, every single summer.
So that's very cool.
What a great tradition.
It's a beautiful place.
It is.
Yeah.
Yeah, I grew up in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania and then I've been in Arizona, uh, for the last, like, 14 years.
But, yeah, still make it back that way every summer.
It's, it's, it's a good tradition, like you said.
So, well, I appreciate you jumping on.
I was talking to Ben.
He was saying that he was kind of filling me in that.
I guess this isn't necessarily the right time for this, but just kind of, kind of give you a high level of what we do
for meeting.
And I totally, in setting up a recurring call just later,
May early June that would be.
Yeah, very cool.
Um, I guess did Ben, uh, did he tell you much about us or do you know, kind of the highlights of what we do?
You know?
I think he's giving some brief scenarios and I appreciate that, but, um, I, I have not retained it to be.
So I'll let you know we're a B I tool.
We provide you with market information, all of your competitors.
So think market surveys, comp data.
Um I always say there's four things that make our product really unique.
So first off everything we do is 100% automated.
So there's zero manual, you know, processes whatsoever.
So, you know, we're not making phone calls, we're not relying on your on site teams to go out there and collect information all automated.
Um The second thing and maybe the best thing is that we actually update our data every single day.
So as I'm sure you're aware, properties out there are using rev tools, they're changing rates regularly.
So our goal is for you to be able to jump into our platform and get that true reflection of what's going on in the marketplace today.
Uh What else is nice is that we can track any property out there as long as they have a website and use a property management software, they do not have to be our current customers.
So we can really track the entire market.
And lastly, what I think is great is that everything that we have in apartment IQ is 100% publicly available data.
The reason I mentioned that is just because of the, the recent, you know, real pages.
Now, you already lawsuits centered on private semi, private data.
Um We have nothing to do with that whatsoever, so completely immune to, to all the legalities there.
Um, I guess to kind of help me better understand what, what's your team's doing right now as far as uh market surveys go,
are getting that comp data.
Yeah.
Um so we have, um had a long partnership with co star and the market service that they provide um and real page and I'm pulling a blank on what it is.

We've used them as well and sometimes we've used both, you know, because they pull different data and, and uh because we, we primarily are property management, but we do have still just a little bit of commercial um properties um that we, you know, we rely on both those tools to be able to feed us the best data.

But, um it's funny, I mean, it's not funny you mentioned, but just, um we have a leadership call every Monday morning and on that call this past Monday, I mean, our executive Vice President, Susan Passmore, she was letting us know, hey, if there are properties because it is such a habit to want to reach out to your competitors and send out that market comp survey.
She's like we need to stop that.
You know, we don't need to be sharing that type of data.
You don't need to be asking for that type of data.
Um Because of the lawsuit mostly.
So, yeah, and you're not alone there.
I've got a list right now of nine of the top 30 PM CS in the country that are no longer participating whatsoever either.
So it is going to be harder and harder to get that.
So our, our solution has become pretty awesome.
Um

I mean, I'll let you know, we just, I mean, honest, I mean, and I'm, I'm willing to take all the notes because I'm a pretty good note taker but, and I thought it was being recorded so you could send that to me as well.

Um But Susan Passmore, as well as Gina Carter, who's our VP of property management of, of operations and Terry Raglan, the VP of property management, they are should be a part of this conversation too because you're definitely a decision maker in this product and in this space for sure.
No, that makes sense.
And I appreciate the transparency and for what it's worth too with apartment IQ we offer unlimited users.
Um a lot of our customers, I mean, they'll give access to the on site teams, regionals, operations, marketing revenue, senior leaders.

Um I don't know if you guys third party manage, but if you do, I mean, some clients get access um So there's a little bit, I mean, there's a little bit of value that we could provide for each level of the organization.
So, yes.
So the, these calls typically do involve multiple organizations, you know, multiple departments.
Um, what I'll do here and I'll make this pretty brief, but I'll kind of just show you what the product looks like.
I won't get into the weeds.
Um, would obviously love to connect with everybody else and to maybe entice them to get on another call with me.
What I'll, what I can do is I can actually take a handful of your properties and I'll set up like a free trial.
That way you can see the data in action with your property and your competitors.
I think that might, you know, that seeing relevant information is usually a little bit more appealing.
Absolutely.
That sounds wonderful.
Thank you.
Yep, for sure.
Um
In terms of costar, you did mention costar.

So what, what's funny is we don't even necessarily look at them as a competitor, you know, there in our minds, they're kind of more bigger picture like the 10,000 ft overview development acquisitions, whereas we're more for daily decision making.
So, I mean, being in the marketing side, I mean, if you look at their data oftentimes it's 68 weeks out of date.
So they might be feeding you information on a property that's offering a concession or, you know, is that concession still live?
Is it from six weeks ago or, you know, that property doesn't have a concession and now they have, you know, a month free or two months free or whatever.
So with us again, since the data is updated daily, you know exactly what your comps are doing.

Um, we even take the value of those concessions and we take it a step further and we calculate that effective rent and that effective price per foot at the property level at the floor plan level and all the way down to the unit level.
So giving you those numbers that, you know, truly matter.
Um but at the same time too, I mean, understanding context, you know, your comps are they, are they offering to waive that fees and admin fees or are they offering two months free?
So we can show you all that, which is great.
Um And then pricing availability too.
Just understanding, you know, the the context, you know, what's their exposure percentage?
Do they have 10 1 bedrooms available?
Do they have 21 bedrooms available?
You know, how's their price fluctuating all that good stuff?
So lots of information for sure.
But yeah, let me show it to you.
Like I said, I'll, I'll give you the three minute high level here.
Um This would be the home screen, the way it's set up is that each one of your properties actually gets its own individual property card.
And then we run the analytics based off of that property specific concept.
So all competitors specific data inside of each property's uh concept.
But if you click into a property,
you basically have full reports associated with each property.
The first report, which we're on here is the market survey.
And then if you select the data drop down, there's three additional reports as well.
This data gets as granular as the unit level.
So I can actually show you every single unit that's currently available in your comp set.
Um Right now for what it's worth, I don't know if you do.
You guys, excuse me, do you guys use a B I tool right now?
Like power B I or clicks sense.
We do, I was just gonna say we, we do use um real pages B I.
So we do have um a couple of different ways that we can adjust or that we can get that data for you to adjust into your B I tool, whether it be CS V file API or uh an endpoint.
So we can get you all the, all the granular data, which is great.
But I mean, looking at the uh the market survey here, I think it's pretty standard and representative of your standard market survey.
Um we list all of your competitors across the top and then we also include your property here.
That way you can easily make comparisons.
Then all the data is accessible via vertical scrolling.

So two sections the first one being high level property summary, you know, we'll show you how far away is each property, uh each competitor from your property you built total number of units, occupancy percentage, the number of units that are available in the property of those units that are available.
How many are vacant?
Down below here?
This is where we'll let you know if there is a rent special or concession attached to the property or attached to a unit in the property.
And lastly, we share some transactional data as well.
So the number of leases that each property had over the last 30 days over the last seven and the number of leases that each property averages per week
um for the sake of the conversation too around public and private data.

The way we, the way we report this transactional data is since we're not actually integrating with the property management software, the way that we track that is since we're tracking each unit individually.
You know, for example, if one, if unit 123 is available today and tomorrow, unit 123 is not, we assume that it has been leased.
So that's how we present this in a publicly available way.
Yep.
What's cool too is I mean, even talking the automation at the top, if you click in any of these images, we pull up the property, draw down, we tell you more about that competitor.
So we'll show you his address we'll show you, you know who manages them.
We give you a link to the website, link to the Google Maps.
And then down below here, we'll show you every single unit that's currently being advertised as available in the property right now.
But yeah, down here pricing availability then just broken up by floor plan.
I mean, the idea here is to understand, you know, where, where our one bedroom position compared to the competition.
And inside of here, I think we give you every key metric to help understand that.

So, you know, gross rent, we let you know if there's a concession attached to this floor plan, net effective rent, square footage price per foot and net effective price per foot and the number of one bedrooms that each property has available.

So being able to sort this from high to low understanding, hey, which property out there has the most expensive one bedrooms, which property has the least expensive one bedrooms and where we fall accordingly?
So are we in line with our strategy?
Are we worried about pushing or are we worried about occupancy?
Are we looking to push rent rates?
So knowing where the ceiling and the floor is and kind of what the market is willing to bear?
Um We do have some reporting as well.
So first off everything I'm showing you is exportable to CS B or to excel.

We also have um we call these like client ownership reports but they're, they're downloadable anytime you want, you could also automate them to be delivered to an inbox on whatever cadence you'd like.
But this is kind of what they look like.
Um They're property specific.
Um you know, we'll give you high level market summary information, high level property insights.
And then we also do a unit type analysis for every bedroom that's available in your comp set.
So what are the averages for every one bedroom available as well as showing you which properties have one bedrooms available and then the data specific to their one bedrooms.
And then lastly, you know, we'll show you uh concession information.
So how many properties in your comp set are offering concessions?
What's the average value of those concessions?
And then down below here, the details for each property offering concessions
dumb.
But that's kind of the high level.
I mean, like I said, I can drag you through the weeds, but I don't think it's necessary for right now.
I just wanted to kind of give you the glimpse of, of what we can do and um, I can send you over collateral.
I can get you, you know, some information to share internally.
Ok.
All right.
That sounds great.
Cool.
But yeah, as far as kind of that next call to everybody else does like May June, does that make sense for that?
I mean, now you got me my wheels turning and I'm not sure I just because it is a relevant topic.
Um Let me, let me speak with Susan.
She's who I report to directly.
She's our executive vice president and she would make the like the final decision on this as well.
Let me speak with her and, and I can get a conversation with her later this afternoon, first of tomorrow and just let her know that we've touched base and see what she feels is best.
She may say honestly, Justin that because of everything on the horizon, potentially, um that would, she would rather go ahead and explore an option.
So again, let me, let me speak with her and let her let her know two things.
One if, if she wants, give me five of your properties and their competitors and I'll set you up a free trial.
II, I think the the quality of our data is I'll take it up against anybody.
So, I mean, you can compare to what you're getting right now from, from co star or from whoever.
Um Second thing too, as far as I know, it's a pain in the butt to work with different vendors and stuff.
But as far as like set up goes and implementation, there's zero integrations.
So on boarding a portfolio is as easy as giving us your properties, their websites and the competitors and we build everything.

Um So just for context, we, we uh we on boarded a uh a 1000 property uh portfolio at the beginning of the year and we had all pro every, every all, all 1000 plus those properties on board by the end of January.
So it's an inanely quick.
Um we offer training too.
So group sessions, one on one sessions, ongoing training sessions if you get new employees.
So if we try to make it easy.
Yeah, that's amazing.
Um But yeah, that makes sense if you want to talk to her.
Um I'll shoot you an email with that collateral and then you can just respond in that thread whenever you guys have that conversation.
I would love that.
Thank you.
Cool.
Awesome.
Well, I appreciate it.
Um Anything else you can think of?
Oh, great.
Well, I look forward to hearing from you and we'll talk soon.
Thanks.
Bye.
